wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the CheapModels platform. Explain what CheapModels is - a unified AI model management platform that provides cost-effective integration with multiple AI providers through a single interface. Document the installation process for both frontend and backend components, environment setup requirements, and initial configuration steps. Include step-by-step instructions for setting up API keys for different AI providers, creating your first account, and making your first API call. Provide quick start examples showing how to integrate with the OpenAI-compatible API endpoints and use the dashboard interface. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for beginners.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the authentication system in CheapModels. Explain the JWT-based authentication flow, including user registration, login processes, and token management. Document the auth provider component that manages authentication state across the application. Detail the security implementation including password hashing, token expiration, and session handling. Include practical examples of how to implement protected routes, handle authentication errors, and manage user sessions. Explain the relationship between frontend auth context and backend authentication endpoints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Structure
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Next.js application structure. Explain the App Router organization, layout composition patterns, and global configuration. Document how pages are structured, the root layout hierarchy, and global styles management. Detail the integration between frontend and backend services, environment configuration, and build process setup. Include information about the project's modular architecture and how different features are organized within the app directory.

wiki_catalogs.name: Service Layer Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the backend service layer design. Describe the service-oriented architecture patterns, module organization, and inter-service communication. Document the provider abstraction layer that handles multiple AI service integrations including OpenAI, Anthropic, and other providers. Explain the conversation management service, request routing, and response handling mechanisms. Detail the error handling strategies, logging implementation, and monitoring approaches used throughout the service layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the main dashboard overview page. Document the dashboard layout structure, navigation components, and key statistics display. Explain how users can access different sections from the main dashboard, view quick actions, and monitor system status. Include details about the responsive design, user interface elements, and integration with other dashboard features. Provide examples of common dashboard workflows and best practices for efficient usage.

wiki_catalogs.name: OpenAI Compatibility Layer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the OpenAI compatibility layer in CheapModels. Explain how the platform provides OpenAI-compatible API endpoints that allow seamless migration from existing OpenAI clients. Document the /api/v1/chat/completions endpoint, request/response formats, and parameter mappings. Include step-by-step migration guides showing before/after code examples for popular OpenAI SDKs in JavaScript, Python, and other languages. Detail authentication differences, error response formats, and streaming capabilities. Address rate limiting, cost optimization strategies when switching providers, and troubleshooting common migration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document the login endpoint (/api/auth/login) with request/response schemas including username/password fields, JWT token structure, and error codes (401, 403). Detail the signup endpoint (/api/auth/signup) with user registration fields, validation rules, and account creation responses. Include authentication flow examples, token refresh mechanisms, session management, and security considerations like password hashing and rate limiting. Provide code examples in multiple languages demonstrating proper authentication implementation.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Primitives
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core UI primitive components built with Shadcn/ui. Document the base component architecture, prop interfaces, styling patterns, and customization options. Explain how primitives serve as building blocks for higher-level components. Include detailed examples of button variants, form inputs, typography elements, and layout primitives. Document accessibility features, responsive behavior, and theme integration. Provide guidelines for extending primitives and maintaining consistency across the component library.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for implementing custom AI providers. Explain the provider abstraction layer architecture, interface requirements, and implementation patterns. Document how to extend the existing provider system to support new AI services. Include step-by-step guides for creating custom providers, handling authentication, managing API keys, and implementing streaming responses. Provide concrete examples of provider implementations, error handling strategies, and testing approaches. Cover provider configuration, rate limiting, and cost tracking integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Usage Metrics & Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the usage metrics and monitoring system. Explain how usage data is collected, processed, and displayed across different AI providers. Document the metrics available including token usage, request counts, response times, and cost calculations. Detail the real-time monitoring capabilities, alerting thresholds, and performance tracking features. Include examples of interpreting usage patterns, identifying bottlenecks, and optimizing API calls for better performance and cost efficiency.

# Comprehensive Project Analysis: CheapModels

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
CheapModels is a modern AI model management platform built with Next.js that provides developers with an affordable and streamlined interface to access various AI models through a unified API. The platform serves as both a frontend dashboard and backend service for managing AI model providers, API keys, usage analytics, and chat interactions.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Cost Optimization**: Provide affordable access to multiple AI model providers
- **Unified Interface**: Centralized dashboard for managing AI resources
- **Developer Experience**: Streamlined API integration with OpenAI-compatible endpoints
- **Usage Analytics**: Comprehensive tracking and monitoring of AI model usage
- **Multi-Provider Support**: Integration with various AI model providers

### Target Audience
- Developers seeking cost-effective AI model access
- Teams requiring centralized AI resource management
- Applications needing OpenAI-compatible API endpoints
- Users wanting to compare and switch between different AI providers

## 2. Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern full-stack architecture with Next.js App Router, featuring a clear separation between frontend and backend concerns while maintaining tight integration for optimal performance.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Next.js Frontend] --> B[API Routes]
    B --> C[Backend Service Layer]
    C --> D[Database Layer]
    C --> E[External AI Providers]
    
    F[Dashboard UI] --> G[Authentication]
    F --> H[Model Management]
    F --> I[Analytics Dashboard]
    F --> J[API Key Management]
    
    K[Chat Interface] --> L[Streaming API]
    L --> M[OpenAI Compatible Endpoint]
    
    N[CLI Interface] --> O[Command Processing]
    O --> P[Backend API]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Frontend Architecture:**
- **App Router Structure**: Utilizes Next.js 14+ App Router with file-based routing
- **Component Organization**: Modular component structure with core UI primitives and feature-specific components
- **State Management**: React Context for theme and authentication state
- **Styling**: CSS Modules with custom design system

**Backend Architecture:**
- **Service Layer**: TypeScript-based backend services handling business logic
- **API Gateway**: Route handlers for RESTful API endpoints
- **Authentication**: JWT-based user authentication system
- **Database Integration**: Structured data access patterns

### Design Patterns

1. **Monorepo Pattern**: Separate `backend/` directory with independent Node.js service
2. **Feature-Based Organization**: Components grouped by functionality rather than type
3. **Context Provider Pattern**: Theme and auth state management
4. **Route Handler Pattern**: Next.js API routes for server-side logic
5. **Service Layer Pattern**: Separation of concerns in backend services

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant NextJS
    participant APIRoutes
    participant Backend
    participant Database
    participant AIProviders
    
    Client->>+NextJS: HTTP Request
    NextJS->>+APIRoutes: Route Handler
    APIRoutes->>+Backend: Service Call
    Backend->>+Database: Data Query
    Database-->>-Backend: Response
    Backend->>+AIProviders: API Request
    AIProviders-->>-Backend: Model Response
    Backend-->>-APIRoutes: Processed Data
    APIRoutes-->>-NextJS: JSON Response
    NextJS-->>-Client: Final Response
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Frontend Application:**
- `src/app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with providers and global configuration
- `src/app/page.tsx` - Landing page implementation
- `src/app/chat/page.tsx` - Chat interface main entry point

**Backend Service:**
- `backend/src/index.ts` - Main server entry point
- `backend/package.json` - Backend dependencies and scripts

### Core Modules

**Authentication System:**
- `src/components/auth-provider.tsx` - Authentication context provider
- `backend/src/auth.ts` - Server-side authentication logic
- `src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts` - Login API endpoint
- `src/app/api/auth/signup/route.ts` - Registration API endpoint

**Database Layer:**
- `src/lib/db.ts` - Database connection and utilities
- `backend/src/db.ts` - Backend database operations
- `backend/src/conversations.ts` - Conversation management

**API Integration:**
- `src/lib/api.ts` - Frontend API client utilities
- `backend/src/providers.ts` - AI provider abstraction layer
- `backend/src/keys.ts` - API key management

### Configuration Approach

**Theme Configuration:**
- `src/config/theme.ts` - Design system and theme definitions
- `components.json` - Shadcn/ui component configuration
- `next.config.ts` - Next.js application configuration

**Environment Management:**
- `backend/src/keys.ts` - Environment variable handling
- `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration for both frontend and backend

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies:**
- Next.js 14+ with App Router
- React 18+ with concurrent features
- Tailwind CSS for styling
- Shadcn/ui for component library
- Lucide React for icons

**Backend Dependencies:**
- Bun runtime for high-performance execution
- TypeScript for type safety
- Database ORM (likely Prisma or similar)
- JWT for authentication
- AI SDK integrations

### Integration Points

**AI Provider Integration:**
- `backend/src/providers.ts` - Multi-provider abstraction
- `src/app/api/v1/chat/completions/route.ts` - OpenAI-compatible endpoint
- `src/app/api/stream/route.ts` - Streaming response handler

**Analytics Integration:**
- `src/app/api/analytics/route.ts` - Usage tracking endpoint
- `src/app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx` - Analytics dashboard

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Layout] --> B[AuthProvider]
    A --> C[ThemeProvider]
    A --> D[SiteNav]
    
    E[Dashboard] --> F[Analytics]
    E --> G[Keys Management]
    E --> H[Providers]
    E --> I[Billing]
    
    J[Chat Page] --> K[MessageList]
    J --> L[InputForm]
    J --> M[StreamingHandler]
    
    N[Landing Page] --> O[HeroSection]
    N --> P[Features]
    N --> Q[Pricing]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**User Management:**
- User registration and authentication system
- Profile management and settings
- Role-based access control

**AI Model Management:**
- Multi-provider support (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
- API key management and rotation
- Model selection and configuration
- Cost optimization strategies

**Chat Interface:**
- Real-time streaming responses
- Conversation history
- Model comparison capabilities
- Export and sharing features

**Analytics Dashboard:**
- Usage tracking and metrics
- Cost analysis and reporting
- Performance monitoring
- Provider comparison tools

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-time Chat System:**
- `src/app/chat/page.tsx` - Main chat interface
- `src/app/api/stream/route.ts` - Server-side streaming
- `src/app/api/v1/chat/completions/route.ts` - OpenAI-compatible API

**Dashboard Components:**
- `src/app/dashboard/analytics/page.tsx` - Analytics visualization
- `src/app/dashboard/keys/page.tsx` - API key management
- `src/app/dashboard/providers/page.tsx` - Provider configuration

**UI Component Library:**
- `src/components/ui/primitives.tsx` - Base UI components
- `src/components/core/text-loop.tsx` - Animated text effects
- `src/components/HeroTerminal.tsx` - Interactive terminal demo

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Authenticated: Login/Signup
    Authenticated --> Dashboard: Navigate
    Authenticated --> Chat: Start Conversation
    Authenticated --> CLI: Use Command Line
    
    Dashboard --> Analytics: View Reports
    Dashboard --> Keys: Manage API Keys
    Dashboard --> Providers: Configure Models
    
    Chat --> Streaming: Send Message
    Streaming --> Response: Receive Answer
    Response --> Chat: Display Result
    
    CLI --> Commands: Execute Commands
    Commands --> Results: Get Output
```

**Authentication Flow:**
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Login] --> B[Validate Credentials]
    B --> C{Valid?}
    C -->|Yes| D[Generate JWT Token]
    C -->|No| E[Show Error]
    D --> F[Store Token]
    F --> G[Redirect to Dashboard]
    E --> A
```

**Chat Streaming Architecture:**
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant C as Chat Component
    participant S as Stream API
    participant B as Backend
    participant P as AI Provider
    
    U->>C: Send Message
    C->>S: POST /api/stream
    S->>B: Forward Request
    B->>P: AI Model Request
    P-->>B: Stream Tokens
    B-->>S: Forward Tokens
    S-->>C: SSE Events
    C-->>U: Update UI
```
